hint: A food made from flour and water.
example: I want a piece of bread with butter.

hint: The clear liquid that we drink to stay alive.
example: Can I have a glass of water, please?

hint: The feeling you have when you smile.
example: Today is a very happy day for me.

hint: To use a pen or pencil to make words on paper.
example: I need to write a letter to my friend.

hint: Not big; little in size.
example: My cat is very small and cute.

hint: A piece of furniture used for sitting.
example: Please sit down on the blue chair.

hint: To get new knowledge or skills.
example: I want to learn English this year.

hint: The color of grass and most trees.
example: The leaves on the tree are green.

hint: A place where children go to study.
example: The children are at school right now.

hint: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow it.
example: Do you want to drink some orange juice?

hint: An opening in a wall to let light and air in.
example: Please close the window; it is cold.

hint: A female parent.
example: My mother is a very kind woman.

hint: Apples, bananas, and oranges are types of this.
example: I usually eat fruit after lunch.

hint: To rest with your eyes closed at night.
example: I need to sleep eight hours every night.

hint: A piece of furniture with four legs used for eating.
example: The dinner is ready on the table.

hint: An animal that has feathers and can fly.
example: A small bird is singing in the tree.

hint: Moving or doing something very fast.
example: Be quick or we will miss the bus!

hint: A building where a family lives.
example: My house is near the park.

hint: To make sounds because something is funny.
example: The children laugh at the funny movie.

hint: The time when it is dark and people sleep.
example: The moon is very bright tonight.

hint: A structure built over a river or road.
example: We walked across the bridge to the city.

hint: To stop an event from happening.
example: They had to cancel the meeting today.

hint: Known by many people.
example: He is a famous actor in Hollywood.

hint: The act of traveling from one place to another.
example: It was a long journey by train.

hint: The room where you cook food.
example: Mom is preparing soup in the kitchen.

hint: Good for your body.
example: Eating vegetables is very healthy.

hint: To take something and give it back later.
example: Can I borrow your pen for a second?

hint: When there are many clouds in the sky.
example: It is a cloudy day, but it is not raining.

hint: To pick one thing from a group.
example: You can choose between tea or coffee.

hint: Something that can hurt you.
example: The sign warns people about the danger.

hint: To not remember something.
example: Don't forget to lock the door.

hint: A period of time for learning a subject.
example: The English lesson starts at nine.

hint: A place where people buy and sell food.
example: I buy fresh fruit at the local market.

hint: New and not using old styles.
example: The office is in a very modern building.

hint: Having good manners toward others.
example: It is important to be polite to everyone.

hint: To look for something carefully.
example: I need to search for my lost keys.

hint: To go to a different city or country.
example: I want to travel to Japan next summer.

hint: Helping you to do or achieve something.
example: This app is very useful for learning.

hint: A very small town in the countryside.
example: He lives in a quiet village in the mountains.

hint: The state of the air (rain, sun, wind).
example: What is the weather like today?

hint: An opinion about what someone should do.
example: Thank you for the good advice.

hint: To think that something is true.
example: I believe that you can pass the test.

hint: A state of physical ease and freedom from pain.
example: I like this sofa because of its comfort.

hint: To make a choice after thinking.
example: It is difficult to decide which one to buy.

hint: Physical or mental energy used to do something.
example: She put a lot of effort into her project.

hint: The power to act, speak, or think as you want.
example: The people fought for their freedom.

hint: Feeling bad because you did something wrong.
example: I felt guilty for missing your party.

hint: To make something better than before.
example: You need to practice to improve your skills.

hint: Fairness in the way people are treated.
example: The lawyer fought for justice in court.

hint: Information and skills gained through experience.
example: Books are a great source of knowledge.

hint: To be in control or charge of something.
example: How do you manage your time so well?

hint: Usual, typical, or expected.
example: It is normal to feel nervous before a test.

hint: A thought or belief about something.
example: In my opinion, this is the best option.

hint: To say that you will certainly do something.
example: I promise to be there on time.

hint: To make something smaller or less.
example: We need to reduce the amount of waste.

hint: The achievement of an aim or purpose.
example: Hard work is the key to success.

hint: An idea or opinion produced by thinking.
example: The thought of flying makes me happy.

hint: Requiring immediate action or attention.
example: I have an urgent message for the boss.

hint: An act of winning a game or contest.
example: The team celebrated their big victory.

hint: To want to know more about something.
example: I wonder why the sky is blue.

hint: Correct, exact, and without any mistakes.
example: The weather forecast was very accurate.

hint: A line that marks the limits of an area.
example: The river forms a natural boundary.

hint: A task that tests someone's ability.
example: Climbing the mountain was a huge challenge.

hint: To grow or change into something more advanced.
example: Scientists want to develop a new medicine.

hint: Facts that show that something is true.
example: There is no evidence to support that claim.

hint: Happening or doing something often.
example: He is a frequent visitor to this library.

hint: To bring things or people together.
example: We need to gather more information first.

hint: Traditions and objects passed down through generations.
example: The castle is part of the national heritage.

hint: To become larger in amount or size.
example: Prices continue to increase every year.

hint: The ability to make considered decisions.
example: You should use your own judgment here.

hint: The quality of being friendly and generous.
example: I will never forget your kindness.

hint: Time when one is not working or occupied.
example: I enjoy painting in my leisure time.

hint: To keep something in good condition.
example: It is hard to maintain a large garden.

hint: Expressing or meaning the answer no.
example: Stop focusing on the negative things.

hint: Easy to see or understand.
example: The answer to the question was obvious.

hint: The force produced by pressing on something.
example: I feel a lot of pressure at work lately.

hint: Something done in response to an event.
example: What was his reaction to the news?

hint: Relating clearly to one particular thing.
example: Could you be more specific about the plan?

hint: To say you will cause trouble if you don't get your way.
example: The dark clouds threaten to bring rain.

hint: Worth a lot of money or very important.
example: She gave me some valuable advice.

hint: Existing in thought but not having physical form.
example: The concept of beauty is quite abstract.

hint: To publicly support a particular policy or cause.
example: They advocate for better human rights.

hint: Relating to the process of thought.
example: The child is showing rapid cognitive growth.

hint: To show or represent by a drawing or painting.
example: The paintings depict life in the old city.

hint: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing.
example: She gave an eloquent speech at the ceremony.

hint: Possible to do easily or conveniently.
example: The plan is economically feasible.

hint: Truly what something is said to be; authentic.
example: He has a genuine interest in local history.

hint: A system where members are ranked by status.
example: The company has a very rigid hierarchy.

hint: Existing in something as a permanent attribute.
example: There are inherent risks in this investment.

hint: Conforming to the law or to rules.
example: The government is considered legitimate.

hint: To make something bad less severe or painful.
example: Steps were taken to mitigate the damage.

hint: A conception of or belief about something.
example: I have no notion of what he means.

hint: Not discovered or known about; uncertain.
example: The origins of the custom are obscure.

hint: A typical example or pattern of something.
example: There is a new paradigm in modern physics.

hint: A state of perplexity or uncertainty.
example: He was in a quandary about which job to take.

hint: No longer needed or useful; superfluous.
example: The extra safety system is redundant.

hint: Critical observation or examination.
example: The proposal is under close scrutiny.

hint: Perceptible by touch; clear and real.
example: The silence was almost tangible.

hint: To be the cause or basis of something.
example: Many factors underlie the current crisis.

hint: Able to adapt or be used in many ways.
example: He is a versatile actor who plays many roles.
